title: Lee Kuan Yew School Youth Development Internship 2026: A Gateway to Global Public Policy Careers
summary: The Lee Kuan Yew School of Public Policy (LKY School), part of the prestigious National University of Singapore , continues to shape future leaders and changemakers through its globally recognized academic and professional programmes. Since its establishment in 2004, the School has become a central hub for public policy innovation, particularly across Asia. Located within the iconic Singapore Botanic Gardens , the LKY School provides a dynamic learning environment supported by cutting-edge research centres that address pressing global and regional challenges. These include geopolitics, economic development, public health, urban planning, environmental sustainability, and education. As part of its commitment to nurturing the next generation of leaders, the LKY School offers the Executive Education (EE) Youth Development Internship 2026 , a unique opportunity for aspiring professionals to gain hands-on experience in public policy and international affairs. About the Executive Education Internship Programme The Executive Education arm of LKY School is designed for professionals seeking to enhance their leadership and technical capabilities through flexible, high-impact programmes. Among its offerings, the Youth Development Internship stands out as an entry point for young talents eager to explore careers in policy, governance, and global affairs. This internship is tailored for individuals who want to bridge academic knowledge with practical exposure. Interns will be immersed in real-world policy environments, contributing to impactful projects while interacting with international experts, policymakers, and professionals. What Makes This Internship Unique The Youth Development Internship goes beyond administrative support—it offers a comprehensive experience that blends learning, networking, and professional growth: Exposure to global public policy frameworks and decision-making processes Direct involvement in executive education programme delivery Opportunities to collaborate with international stakeholders Participation in meaningful, real-world projects Insight into leadership development and capacity-building initiatives Interns will also gain access to LKY School’s flagship programmes, including the renowned Senior Management Programme, allowing them to observe high-level leadership training in action. Key Responsibilities As an intern, you will play a vital role in ensuring the success of executive education programmes. Your tasks will include: Providing administrative and logistical support for programme delivery Assisting in coordination with faculty members and international clients Supporting programme assessments and post-programme evaluations Liaising with external vendors to manage logistics Organizing meetings and preparing reports and documentation Contributing to special projects assigned by the department This role requires adaptability, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple responsibilities in a fast-paced environment. Skills and Competencies You Will Develop Through this internship, participants will build a strong foundation in: Policy analysis and research Professional communication and stakeholder engagement Project management and coordination Critical thinking and problem-solving Cross-cultural collaboration These competencies are essential for careers in international organizations, government institutions, NGOs, and global think tanks. Eligibility Criteria To be considered for this internship, applicants must: Be a current student or recent graduate from a post-secondary or tertiary institution in Singapore Demonstrate a strong interest in public policy or international affairs Be available full-time for at least 3 months (April to July 2026) , with possible extension Possess strong analytical, writing, and communication skills Show initiative, adaptability, and a willingness to learn Be detail-oriented and capable of working independently Be based in Singapore Internship Benefits Participants in the programme will receive: A monthly allowance Hands-on experience in a globally recognized policy institution Exposure to international networks and professionals Practical skills that enhance employability A strong foundation for future careers in public policy and international relations Why You Should Apply If you are passionate about shaping public policy, contributing to global development, and building a meaningful career in international affairs, this internship offers a powerful starting point. The LKY School Youth Development Internship is not just a learning experience—it is a stepping stone into the world of global governance, diplomacy, and leadership. Application Timeline Applicants are expected to commit between April and July 2026 , with flexibility for extension depending on performance and availability. For more information click here APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities here The post Lee Kuan Yew School Youth Development Internship 2026: A Gateway to Global Public Policy Careers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: Zero Waste Academy Africa 2026 – Climate & Waste Management Training for Changemakers
summary: The Zero Waste Academy Africa 2026 , organized by Global Alliance for Incinerator Alternatives , is a capacity-building program designed to equip individuals and institutions with the knowledge and practical skills needed to implement zero waste solutions across African communities. This training initiative focuses on addressing critical environmental challenges such as plastic pollution, poor waste management systems, and methane emissions. Through structured learning and practical insights, participants will gain the tools needed to drive sustainable change in their local contexts. About the Opportunity The Zero Waste Academy Africa is a structured training platform aimed at strengthening the capacity of climate advocates, practitioners, policymakers, and organizations working in environmental sustainability. Rooted in successful models from the Asia-Pacific region, the academy has been adapted to address the specific challenges faced across Africa, particularly in relation to solid waste management and methane abatement. Participants will explore key topics such as: Movement building for climate action Integration of waste pickers into formal systems Waste characterization and data collection Composting and organic waste solutions Designing effective waste collection and segregation strategies The program combines theoretical learning with practical applications, ensuring that participants not only understand the concepts but are also able to implement them effectively in their communities. The academy also emphasizes collaboration and shared learning, bringing together individuals from different sectors to exchange ideas and develop context-specific solutions. What You’ll Gain The Zero Waste Academy Africa offers participants valuable skills and exposure that can directly support their work in sustainability and environmental management: Practical knowledge of zero waste principles and implementation strategies Understanding of methane abatement and its role in climate action Skills in designing and managing waste systems at the community or municipal level Exposure to real-world case studies and best practices Opportunity to connect with a network of climate advocates and practitioners Tools to develop scalable and sustainable waste solutions This program is especially beneficial for those looking to move from theory to action in addressing environmental challenges. Eligibility The academy is open to a broad range of participants across Africa who are engaged or interested in waste management and climate action. Eligible applicants include: Climate advocates and environmental activists Practitioners working in sustainability or waste management Policymakers and government officials, particularly at the municipal level Civil society organizations and NGOs Representatives from public and private institutions Applicants should have a demonstrated interest in improving waste management systems and contributing to environmental sustainability in their communities. Why This Opportunity Matters Waste management is one of the most pressing environmental challenges in Africa, with increasing levels of plastic waste, limited infrastructure, and rising methane emissions contributing to climate change. The Zero Waste Academy Africa addresses these challenges by focusing on practical, community-driven solutions. Rather than relying on large-scale infrastructure alone, the program emphasizes approaches that are locally adaptable, cost-effective, and sustainable. Zero waste strategies not only reduce environmental harm but also create economic opportunities, improve public health, and strengthen community resilience. By equipping participants with the right tools and knowledge, the academy plays a key role in advancing these outcomes. This initiative also contributes to a broader movement toward sustainable development across the continent, empowering individuals and organizations to take action and drive systemic change. Application Process and Deadline Interested applicants are required to submit an application through the official Zero Waste Africa platform. The selection process is competitive, and only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Applicants are encouraged to clearly demonstrate their interest in zero waste solutions and how they plan to apply the knowledge gained through the program. Deadline: 10 April 2026 To learn more, visit the official GAIA Zero Waste Academy website . You can use this form to apply/ Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Gender + AI Reporting Fellowship 2026 in Kenya: Fully Funded Opportunity for Women Journalists to Explore AI, Technology, and Gender
summary: The Gender + AI Reporting Fellowship 2026 , organized by the Africa Women Journalism Project (AWJP) in partnership with DW Akademie, is now open for applications. This highly impactful six-month fellowship is designed to empower women journalists in Kenya to investigate and report on the intersection of artificial intelligence, technology, and gender. As AI continues to influence sectors such as governance, healthcare, finance, education, and digital services, this fellowship provides a unique platform for journalists to critically examine how these technologies affect women and gender-diverse communities. It also equips participants with the tools and skills needed to responsibly use AI in journalism. This opportunity is ideal for journalists seeking to deepen their expertise, produce meaningful stories, and contribute to conversations shaping the future of technology and society. About the Gender + AI Reporting Fellowship The Gender + AI Reporting Fellowship builds on the success of its first cohort, which supported women journalists in producing impactful stories on emerging technologies and gender issues. The 2026 cohort aims to select 10 women journalists who are passionate about storytelling, investigative journalism, and exploring the societal implications of AI. Participants will gain hands-on experience, mentorship, and access to resources that support high-quality journalism. Fellowship Duration and Structure Duration: April – September 2026 (6 months) The fellowship is structured into three key phases: 1. Training Phase Participants will engage in intensive online training sessions covering: AI literacy for journalists Gender-sensitive reporting Investigating the social impacts of AI Ethical and responsible use of AI tools Reporting on data, algorithms, and digital systems These sessions will be facilitated by AWJP, DW Akademie, experienced journalists, and AI experts. 2. Mentorship Each fellow will receive one-on-one mentorship from experienced editors and journalists. Mentorship includes: Story development guidance Editorial feedback Support during reporting and production 3. Story Production Fellows will: Develop and publish four original journalism stories Explore how AI impacts women and marginalized communities Use AI tools to enhance reporting Topics may include: Governance Development Finance Education Health Climate The programme aims to produce at least 40 stories across Kenyan media outlets . 4. Showcase and Networking At the end of the fellowship: Fellows will present their work in a public showcase Opportunities will be created to connect with media organizations and editors What the Fellowship Offers Selected fellows will receive a comprehensive support package: Key Benefits Story production microgrants Access to AI tools for journalism Editorial mentorship from experienced professionals Data analysis and visual storytelling support Networking and professional development opportunities Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Who Can Apply Women journalists based in Kenya Professionals working in print, radio, television, or digital media Freelance journalists are also eligible Requirements Ability to participate in online training and mentorship Access to a laptop and reliable internet connection Demonstrated interest in gender, technology, or digital issues Submission of a letter of intent to publish from a newsroom Priority consideration will be given to journalists from: Nyanza Western Rift Valley (including Turkana) Central Coastal regions Application Requirements Applicants must submit: Curriculum Vitae (maximum 2 pages) Three samples of published work Four story pitches (maximum 800 words total) Motivational statement (maximum 500 words) Signed letter of intent from a media outlet Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated based on: Quality and originality of story ideas Journalism experience Motivation and commitment Interest in gender and technology issues Diversity across regions and media sectors Shortlisted candidates may be invited for a virtual interview. Application Timeline Application Deadline: April 15, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early to increase their chances of selection. How to Apply Interested applicants can complete the Online Application and upload required documents through the official platform. For inquiries: Email: contact@theawjp.org About the Organizers Africa Women Journalism Project (AWJP) AWJP supports women journalists across Africa by providing training, mentorship, and funding opportunities to produce impactful, gender-sensitive reporting. DW Akademie DW Akademie is Deutsche Welle’s international media development center, working globally to strengthen press freedom, journalism quality, and access to information. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 1. Do I need prior experience in AI to apply? No. The fellowship includes training on AI tools and concepts, so prior experience is not required. 2. Can freelance journalists apply? Yes, freelance journalists are eligible. 3. How many fellows will be selected? A total of 10 women journalists will be selected. 4. Is the fellowship fully funded? Yes, it includes microgrants, mentorship, and access to tools and resources. 5. What is the expected output? Each fellow must produce four original journalism stories . 6. Is participation fully online? Yes, training and mentorship are conducted online. 7. Do I need a media outlet commitment? Yes, a signed letter of intent from a newsroom is required. 8. What topics can I cover? Topics may include AI’s impact on governance, education, health, finance, climate, and more. 9. Will there be networking opportunities? Yes, including a final showcase and connections with media professionals. 10. Who organizes the fellowship? It is organized by AWJP in partnership with DW Akademie. Click HERE to learn more about the Gender + AI Reporting Fellowship 2026 in Kenya. Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE and women opportunities HERE. For more digital and AI opportunities on OFY, click HERE. title: Apply Now: Paid GIZ Internship 2026 in Berlin – Indo-German Digital Dialogue
summary: The Indo-German Digital Dialogue Internship offered by Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit is a paid, full-time opportunity based in Berlin . This six-month internship provides hands-on experience in international digital policy, stakeholder engagement, and global project coordination. Designed for students and recent graduates, this internship offers a unique entry point into the world of international cooperation and digital governance, with exposure to high-level policy discussions and cross-country collaboration. About the Opportunity This internship is part of a project commissioned by the German Federal Ministry for Digital Affairs and State Modernisation. The Indo-German Digital Dialogue serves as a platform for collaboration between governments, private sector actors, researchers, and civil society in both Germany and India. The initiative focuses on key areas such as artificial intelligence, telecommunications (including 6G), digital regulation, and industry collaboration . Interns will support these efforts by contributing to research, communication, and coordination activities within an international team. The position is based in Berlin, but the team operates globally, with colleagues located in countries such as India, Brazil, Ghana, Indonesia, Kenya, and Mexico. This creates a highly international working environment where digital collaboration and cross-cultural communication are essential. Due to the distributed nature of the team, interns are expected to work independently, manage tasks proactively, and engage effectively in virtual collaboration. What You’ll Gain This internship offers a strong foundation for careers in international policy, development, and digital governance: Monthly stipend of approximately €2,349 (gross) Direct exposure to international digital policy and cooperation Experience working with government, private sector, and civil society stakeholders Participation in events, delegation visits, and policy-related activities Development of project management and communication skills Opportunity to work in a globally distributed and multicultural team This is particularly valuable for individuals looking to build a career at the intersection of technology, policy, and international development . Eligibility To be eligible for this internship, applicants must meet the following requirements: Be currently enrolled in a university program or in a training program that includes an internship as a mandatory component, or have graduated within the last six months Be studying or have studied fields such as political science, economics, social sciences, communication, or a related discipline Have knowledge or interest in digital economy or digital policy Demonstrate initial experience in communication, public relations, marketing, or journalism Possess excellent German language skills (written and spoken) Have strong English proficiency Show strong organizational, communication, and teamwork skills Be comfortable working independently and in an international environment Applicants should also have good knowledge of standard office software and digital tools. Why This Opportunity Matters As digital technologies continue to shape economies and societies, international cooperation in areas such as artificial intelligence, telecommunications, and digital regulation is becoming increasingly important. The Indo-German Digital Dialogue plays a key role in facilitating this cooperation by bringing together stakeholders from different sectors and countries. This internship allows participants to contribute directly to these efforts, gaining insight into how digital policy is developed and implemented at an international level. It also provides a practical understanding of how global partnerships are managed, how policy discussions are translated into action, and how different actors collaborate to address complex challenges. For early-career professionals, this experience can serve as a stepping stone into careers in international organizations, government agencies, or the private sector. Application Process and Deadline To apply for the internship, candidates must submit their application through the official GIZ careers portal. The application typically includes: Completing the online application form Submitting a CV and supporting documents Demonstrating relevant academic background and experience Applicants may also be contacted for further steps in the selection process. Deadline: 8 April 2026 Internship Period: 1 May 2026 – 30 November 2026 To learn more and apply, visit the official GIZ internship page . title: Apply Now: Plan International Global Young Influencer Group (GYIG) 2026
summary: The Global Young Influencer Group (GYIG) , led by Plan International , is a unique global opportunity for young people aged 15 to 23 to actively shape international campaigns on critical issues such as gender equality and climate justice. This initiative brings together a diverse group of young activists from around the world to contribute to advocacy efforts, influence global conversations, and develop leadership skills. This is not a traditional program or training—it is a youth-led advocacy platform where selected participants directly contribute to real campaigns and initiatives that aim to create global impact. About the Opportunity The Global Young Influencer Group is designed to amplify youth voices in global advocacy. Members of the group work closely with Plan International teams to co-create campaigns, provide insights, and ensure that young people’s perspectives are meaningfully included in decision-making processes. Participants will be involved in shaping upcoming campaigns, particularly those focused on the intersection of climate change and gender equality . This means contributing ideas, reviewing campaign strategies, and helping ensure that messaging reflects the realities and priorities of young people across different regions. The program emphasizes collaboration and diversity. Selected participants will work alongside peers from various countries and backgrounds, creating a space for shared learning and cross-cultural exchange. This collaborative environment helps participants broaden their understanding of global issues while strengthening their ability to advocate effectively. Importantly, the GYIG is structured to ensure that young people are not just participants but active contributors . Their insights and experiences play a central role in shaping campaign strategies and outcomes. Why This Opportunity Matters Young people are increasingly at the forefront of global movements for change, particularly in areas such as climate action and gender equality. However, their voices are not always included in decision-making processes. The Global Young Influencer Group addresses this gap by creating a structured platform where young people can actively contribute to advocacy efforts at an international level. It ensures that campaigns are informed by real experiences and perspectives, making them more relevant and impactful. This opportunity is especially important because it empowers young people to: Influence global conversations on critical issues Develop the skills needed for effective advocacy Build networks that support long-term engagement in social change Contribute to campaigns that can drive policy and societal change By participating in the GYIG, young people gain both the tools and the platform to make their voices heard in meaningful ways. What You’ll Gain Being part of the Global Young Influencer Group offers several meaningful benefits: Direct involvement in global campaigns addressing major social and environmental issues Opportunity to influence advocacy strategies and messaging Experience collaborating with an international network of young leaders Development of advocacy, communication, and campaigning skills Exposure to global policy discussions and youth engagement processes A platform to amplify your voice and contribute to real-world change This opportunity is particularly valuable for young people who want to move beyond awareness and actively participate in shaping solutions. Eligibility The Global Young Influencer Group is open to young people from around the world who are passionate about advocacy and social change. To be eligible, applicants must: Be between 15 and 23 years old Fluent in French, English or Spanish Demonstrate interest in issues such as gender equality, climate justice, and youth rights Be motivated to contribute to global campaigns and collaborative initiatives Be willing to engage with a diverse, international group of peers Plan International strongly encourages applications from individuals of all backgrounds , including those from underrepresented and marginalized communities. The program aims to ensure diverse representation and inclusive participation. Application Process and Deadline The application process for the Global Young Influencer Group is designed to be accessible and flexible. To apply, you need to introduce yourself and explain: Why you want to be part of the group What skills and experience you can bring What you hope to gain from the opportunity This can be submitted in one of two formats: Written submission: Maximum of 1 page Video submission: Maximum of 2 minutes Applicants should choose the format that best allows them to express themselves. Applications must be submitted via email, following the instructions in the official call Deadline: 5 April 2026 To learn more and apply, visit the official Plan International call page. title: Open Society Justice Initiative Democracy Anchor Grants 2026: Up to $500,000 Funding for Strategic Litigation to Defend Democracy
summary: The Open Society Justice Initiative Democracy Anchor Grants 2026 is now open for applications, offering a significant funding opportunity for organizations working at the forefront of defending democracy, human rights, and the rule of law through strategic litigation . With grants of up to $500,000 over three years , this initiative aims to support organizations that are actively challenging democratic backsliding, protecting civic space, and strengthening justice systems worldwide. At a time when democratic institutions face increasing pressure, this programme provides essential resources to organizations driving systemic legal change. About the Open Society Justice Initiative The Open Society Justice Initiative, part of the Open Society Foundations network, has spent over two decades using legal tools to advance open societies, accountability, and justice globally. Through this new grant programme, the initiative seeks to build a resilient global ecosystem of legal actors capable of addressing critical threats to democracy, including electoral interference, judicial repression, and executive overreach. About the Democracy Anchor Grants The Democracy Anchor Grants are designed to support high-impact, strategic litigation efforts that contribute to systemic change. The programme prioritizes organizations that go beyond individual legal cases to create broader societal impact through advocacy, public awareness, and institutional reform. Focus Areas of the Grant Applicants must align their proposals with one or more of the following priority areas: 1. Interference in Democratic Processes Projects addressing: Restrictions on voting rights Barriers to political participation Challenges to election integrity and vote counting Limitations on candidacy and political engagement 2. Threats to Judicial Actors Projects that respond to: Violence or intimidation against judges and legal professionals Political interference in judicial independence 3. Executive Overreach Projects targeting: Abuse of executive powers Suppression of dissent and opposition voices Misuse of emergency powers Violations of freedom of expression and association What the Grant Offers 1. Financial Support Grants of up to $500,000 Funding duration of up to three years Approximately 6–12 organizations will be selected 2. Strategic Impact Opportunities Support for litigation that drives systemic change Strengthening of legal precedents and institutional reforms Opportunities to influence public policy and awareness 3. Ecosystem Development Support for collaboration across legal and advocacy networks Capacity building for strategic litigation organizations Knowledge sharing within the global justice community Eligibility Criteria Organizations must meet the following requirements: Organizational Requirements Must be a nonprofit or charitable organization Demonstrated track record in strategic litigation Must have litigated cases within the past four years Must have at least two legal staff members Strong governance and financial management systems Proven ability to collaborate with other organizations Financial Requirements Annual operating budget below $5 million USD Must have or be seeking funding from non-Open Society sources Cannot have large active Open Society litigation grants exceeding thresholds Additional Considerations Commitment to gender equality and intersectional approaches Inclusion of marginalized communities in litigation strategies Proposal Requirements Applicants must submit proposals that clearly demonstrate: Innovative use of strategic litigation to strengthen democracy A credible plan for broader impact , including: Legal precedent Public awareness Institutional reform Strong community engagement and representation Contribution to strengthening the strategic litigation ecosystem Clear impact measurement and learning strategies Application Timeline Application Deadline: April 27, 2026 (5:00 PM EDT) Applicants should submit their Expression of Interest (EOI) before the deadline. Selection Process and Timeline The selection process follows multiple stages: March 2026: Call for Expressions of Interest opens April 2026: Deadline for submissions May 2026: Review and shortlisting of applicants June 2026: Interviews with shortlisted candidates July 2026: Final proposal submission and compliance review August 2026: Final selection, onboarding, and first disbursement Important Conditions Applications must be submitted in English Only one application per organization is allowed Open Society retains full discretion over funding decisions Funds cannot be used for debt servicing or insolvency coverage Why You Should Apply This grant provides a rare opportunity to: Secure substantial funding for impactful legal work Strengthen democratic institutions and the rule of law Build long-term capacity for strategic litigation Join a global network of organizations defending democracy Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 1. How many grants will be awarded? Approximately 6 to 12 organizations will receive funding. 2. What is considered “strategic litigation”? Strategic litigation combines: Community engagement Legal action in courts Advocacy efforts It must aim for systemic change beyond individual cases . 3. What types of legal actions qualify? Eligible actions include: Litigation in domestic, regional, or international courts Preparation for litigation in restrictive environments Non-binding mechanisms (e.g., UN recommendations only) are not sufficient. 4. Can organizations collaborate on applications? Yes, collaborative applications are allowed, provided all partners meet eligibility criteria. 5. Is sub-granting permitted? Yes, proposals may include sub-granting components. 6. Are fiscal sponsors allowed? Yes, fiscal sponsorship arrangements are accepted. 7. Can organizations apply if they already have Open Society funding? Yes, but only if existing grants do not exceed specified thresholds. 8. Is the grant restricted or unrestricted? Funding may be either restricted or unrestricted, depending on the proposal and organizational needs. 9. Can proposals focus on multiple cases or broader strategies? Yes. Applicants may propose: Individual cases Portfolios of cases Broader strategic litigation initiatives 10. What language should applications be submitted in? Applications must be submitted in English only . How to Apply Interested organizations should submit their Expression of Interest through the Official Open Society platform. For inquiries, applicants may contact the Open Society Justice Initiative directly. Click HERE for more information about the Open Society Justice Initiative Democracy Anchor Grants 2026. Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY HERE. title: Apply Now: Ian Axford Fellowship 2027 in Public Policy
summary: The Ian Axford Fellowship in Public Policy 2027 is a prestigious, fully funded international fellowship designed for mid-career professionals from the United States. Hosted in Wellington , this fellowship offers a unique opportunity to spend 3 to 5 months engaging with public policy in New Zealand while contributing to meaningful policy discussions and research. Delivered through the Ian Axford Fellowships in Public Policy , the program aims to strengthen mutual understanding between the United States and New Zealand by supporting professionals who can bridge ideas, experiences, and policy approaches across both countries. About the Opportunity The Ian Axford Fellowship is designed for professionals who are actively working in public policy or related fields and are interested in gaining international experience. Fellows are placed within New Zealand government agencies and work on policy-focused research projects that address real-world challenges. The fellowship begins with an orientation program in February in Wellington, where fellows are introduced to New Zealand’s policy landscape and institutional environment. During their time in the country, fellows conduct in-depth research, collaborate with local experts, and engage with policymakers. A central component of the fellowship is the development of a policy report , which is later published and shared with relevant stakeholders. Fellows are also expected to present their findings through seminars and public engagements, contributing to broader policy discussions. The program is not limited to a specific policy area. Applicants are encouraged to propose projects aligned with New Zealand’s current policy priorities, but innovative and relevant ideas from other fields are also welcome. Why This Opportunity Matters In an increasingly interconnected world, public policy challenges often require global perspectives and collaboration. The Ian Axford Fellowship plays a critical role in fostering this exchange by bringing US professionals into New Zealand’s policy environment. New Zealand is widely recognized for its innovative approaches to governance, social policy, and public sector management. By immersing fellows in this context, the program enables them to explore alternative approaches, compare systems, and identify transferable lessons. At the same time, fellows contribute their own expertise and perspectives, creating a two-way exchange of knowledge. This not only strengthens bilateral relationships but also enriches policy development in both countries. Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. What You’ll Gain The Ian Axford Fellowship offers a combination of financial support, professional development, and international exposure: Round-trip airfare between the United States and New Zealand Monthly living allowance depending on employment status Additional financial support for fellows with dependents Opportunity to work closely with New Zealand government agencies Experience conducting international policy research Publication of a policy report and opportunities for public engagement Expanded global network in public policy and governance This fellowship provides a rare chance to step outside your usual work environment and gain insights that can directly inform your work back in the United States. Eligibility The fellowship is specifically designed for mid-career professionals from the United States . To be eligible, applicants must: Be a US citizen Have at least five years of professional experience Be actively working in the public, private, or non-profit sector Demonstrate leadership potential and the ability to influence policy Show a strong interest in learning from New Zealand’s policy environment Be able to apply lessons learned to their work in the United States Applicants should also be able to secure support from their employer, including confirmation of whether they will receive full, partial, or no paid leave during the fellowship. Application Process and Deadline Applicants must complete an online application and submit all required materials before the deadline. A strong application should clearly demonstrate how the proposed project will contribute to public policy in both New Zealand and the United States. The application requires: Completed application form Supporting documents outlining your experience and proposed project A letter from your employer confirming support and leave status Three references submitted separately Applications are carefully reviewed, and selected candidates are chosen based on the quality, relevance, and potential impact of their proposed work. Deadline: 10:00 AM (NZT), Tuesday, 21 April 2026 To learn more and apply, visit the official Ian Axford Fellowship website . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: 5th Cohort Policy Analyst Opportunity at CISES: Launch Your Career in Global Policy Research (Remote Role)
summary: The 5th Cohort Policy Analyst application by the Centre for International Security and Economic Strategy (CISES) presents an exceptional opportunity for students and recent graduates seeking hands-on experience in global policy research, analysis, and writing. This remote, flexible, and output-based role is designed to help emerging researchers build expertise, publish their work, and contribute meaningfully to international policy discussions. About CISES The Centre for International Security and Economic Strategy (CISES) is an independent think tank based in London. It focuses on producing high-quality analysis in key areas such as: International security Global economics Defence and strategy Emerging technologies Trade and international law CISES provides a platform for young researchers worldwide to develop their analytical skills, gain practical policy experience, and contribute to real-world conversations shaping global affairs. Role Overview: What Does a Policy Analyst Do? As a Policy Analyst in the 5th cohort, you will actively engage in producing research-driven content on global issues. This role offers both independence and collaboration, allowing you to explore your interests while receiving structured editorial support. Key Responsibilities Policy Analysts are expected to: Conduct independent research on international security, defence, economics, and strategy Pitch and develop original research ideas Produce high-quality written outputs based on selected workload formats Collaborate with fellow analysts in working groups Maintain effective communication and meet agreed deadlines Flexible Workload Structure CISES operates an output-based contribution model, giving you flexibility to choose your workload depending on your schedule. Each month, you are expected to complete one of the following: Four short-form articles , OR Two medium-form articles , OR One long-form article You may also combine formats, such as: Two short-form + one medium-form piece If you are part of a working group: Minimum requirement: One short-form piece per month (unless otherwise agreed) This flexibility makes it ideal for students balancing academic or personal commitments. Who Can Apply? This opportunity is open to: Current undergraduate, postgraduate, or doctoral students Recent graduates (2024 or later) Individuals in at least their second year of tertiary education Essential Skills Qualities Applicants should demonstrate: Strong interest in global affairs, international security, or economics Excellent writing and analytical skills Ability to produce clear, structured, and concise policy content Self-motivation and reliability Ability to meet deadlines consistently While prior research or policy experience is advantageous, it is not mandatory . English Language Requirement Applicants must have professional or native-level English proficiency to ensure high-quality analytical writing. Location Fully remote role Applicants are ideally based in: United Kingdom European Economic Area (EEA) North America Australia / New Zealand Remuneration This is an unpaid (voluntary) role However, the long-term career benefits and exposure make it a valuable stepping stone into global policy careers. Benefits and Career Growth Joining CISES as a Policy Analyst offers several professional advantages: 1. Career Progression Promotion to Senior Policy Analyst after 9 months of consistent contributions 2. Published Work Portfolio All your work remains published under your name , even after leaving 3. Mentorship Skill Development Access to editorial guidance and mentorship Opportunity to refine research, analysis, and writing skills 4. Professional Recognition Receive a LinkedIn recommendation from the Director of Policy after 4–5 months 5. Research Flexibility Freedom to explore topics of interest Ability to shape your own research direction Application Process Timeline Applications are reviewed on a rolling weekly basis until Friday, 1st of May 2026 : Review Day: Every Thursday Decision Notification: Every Friday Why You Should Apply This opportunity is ideal if you are looking to: Build a strong portfolio in international policy research Gain practical experience in a think tank environment Develop critical thinking and analytical writing skills Network with like-minded global researchers Position yourself for careers in policy, research, or international organizations How to Apply To apply for the 5th Cohort Policy Analyst role, visit the official application page below: Apply for the CISES Policy Analyst Programme Learn more about the Policy Analyst Opportunity at CISES HERE. Discover more Policy related roles on OFY HERE. title: UNESCO International Fund for Cultural Diversity (IFCD) 2026: Global Grant Opportunity for Creative and Cultural Sector Development
summary: The UNESCO International Fund for Cultural Diversity (IFCD) 2026 Call for Funding Requests is now officially open, offering a powerful grant opportunity for public institutions and non-governmental organizations (NGOs) working to strengthen cultural and creative industries in developing countries. Launched under the framework of the 2005 Convention on the Protection and Promotion of the Diversity of Cultural Expressions , this global initiative supports projects that drive sustainable cultural development, empower creative entrepreneurs, and enhance access to diverse cultural goods and services. With a strong track record of impact, the IFCD continues to serve as a catalyst for policy innovation, institutional capacity building, and inclusive cultural growth worldwide. About the International Fund for Cultural Diversity (IFCD) The International Fund for Cultural Diversity is a multi-donor fund established by UNESCO to support long-term structural change in the cultural and creative sectors of developing countries. Since its inception in 2010, the IFCD has: Invested over US$13.6 million Funded 175 projects Reached 77 developing countries These projects have contributed significantly to: Strengthening cultural policies Supporting creative entrepreneurship Expanding access to cultural markets Promoting diversity and inclusion in cultural expression Objective of the 2026 Call for Proposals The 2026 IFCD call focuses on projects that deliver sustainable, structural impact in the cultural and creative industries. Proposals should align with one or both of the following priorities: 1. Policy Development and Implementation Projects that: Develop or implement cultural policies and regulatory frameworks Promote diversity in cultural goods and services Improve access, production, and distribution within cultural sectors 2. Capacity Building and Institutional Strengthening Projects that: Strengthen public institutions and civil society organizations Support the development of viable cultural industries and markets Enhance professional skills within the creative economy What the Grant Offers Selected projects will receive financial support to implement initiatives that contribute to cultural development at local, national, or regional levels. Key Benefits Include: Funding for impactful cultural and creative projects Support for policy innovation and implementation Opportunities to strengthen institutional and organizational capacity Contribution to global cultural diversity and inclusion Who Can Apply The IFCD 2026 call is open to: Eligible Applicants Public institutions from eligible developing countries Non-governmental organizations (NGOs) based in eligible countries International NGOs registered in States Parties to the 2005 Convention Applicants must demonstrate the ability to implement projects that create long-term structural change in the cultural sector. Eligible Project Areas Projects should focus on: Cultural policy development and reform Creative industry growth and sustainability Market access for cultural goods and services Cultural entrepreneurship and innovation Inclusion and participation in cultural life Application Timeline Call Opens: March 23, 2026 Application Deadline: May 6, 2026 (12:00 noon Paris time) Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their proposals well before the deadline. How to Apply Interested organizations can access detailed guidelines and submit their proposals through the official UNESCO IFCD portal: Apply for the UNESCO IFCD 2026 Grant For inquiries: Email: convention2005.ifcd@unesco.org Why You Should Apply The UNESCO IFCD Grant is a unique opportunity to: Drive sustainable development in the cultural and creative sectors Influence cultural policy and governance Strengthen institutional capacity and innovation Expand access to cultural markets and opportunities Contribute to global cultural diversity and inclusion For more information about the UNESCO International Fund for Cultural Diversity (IFCD) 2026, visit HERE. Click HERE to discover more global Grant opportunities on OFY. title: Climate Change and Press Freedom Fellowship 2026: $75,400 Paid Global Opportunity with the Committee to Protect Journalists (CPJ)
summary: The Climate Change and Press Freedom Fellowship 2026 , offered by the Committee to Protect Journalists (CPJ), is now open for applications. This prestigious, fully paid, one-year opportunity invites professionals worldwide to contribute to global efforts protecting journalists covering climate change and environmental issues. With a salary of $75,400 (or market equivalent globally) and flexible remote or hybrid work options, this fellowship provides a unique platform to engage in high-impact research, advocacy, and policy development at the intersection of climate change and press freedom. About the Committee to Protect Journalists (CPJ) Founded in 1981, CPJ is an independent, nonprofit organization dedicated to defending journalists and promoting press freedom globally. Over the decades, CPJ has: Secured the release of imprisoned journalists Helped bring perpetrators of journalist killings to justice Advocated for legal reforms protecting free expression Built globally recognized databases and reports on press freedom violations Through its Climate Crisis Journalist Protection Initiative , CPJ is expanding its efforts to address the growing risks faced by journalists reporting on environmental and climate-related issues. About the Fellowship The Climate Change and Press Freedom Fellowship is a 12-month programme designed to support CPJ’s advocacy and research work. The selected fellow will join CPJ’s Global Advocacy team and contribute to developing strategies that protect journalists reporting on climate change, environmental degradation, and related issues such as corruption, corporate accountability, and human rights. Key Responsibilities of the Fellow The fellowship involves a range of impactful research, advocacy, and collaboration activities: 1. Research and Policy Analysis Conduct original research on the intersection of climate change and press freedom Analyze global trends and identify risks faced by environmental journalists Develop policy recommendations and advocacy strategies 2. Data and Landscape Analysis Utilize CPJ’s extensive database spanning over 30 years Conduct cross-regional assessments of threats against journalists Identify key patterns and areas requiring intervention 3. Global Engagement Conduct surveys with environmental journalists worldwide Organize and participate in roundtables and regional meetings Engage with civil society organizations, newsrooms, and advocacy groups 4. Advocacy and Communication Draft reports, briefing materials, and articles Support public and private events Contribute to CPJ’s global advocacy agenda 5. Institutional Contribution Strengthen CPJ’s knowledge base on climate and press freedom Help shape long-term strategies for journalist safety What the Fellowship Offers The programme provides comprehensive professional and financial benefits: 1. Competitive Compensation Annual salary of $75,400 (or equivalent depending on location) 2. Flexible Work Arrangement Remote or hybrid options Open globally (within 6 hours of US Eastern Time) 3. Professional Growth Opportunity to work with a globally recognized organization Exposure to international advocacy and policy work Development of expertise in climate change and press freedom 4. Impactful Career Experience Contribute to global efforts protecting journalists Work on pressing global challenges at the intersection of media and climate Eligibility Criteria Applicants should meet the following requirements: Required Qualifications Strong knowledge of press freedom, journalism, and climate change Demonstrated experience in climate or environmental issues Excellent research and writing skills Strong analytical thinking and attention to detail Preferred Skills Familiarity with corruption, corporate accountability, or investigative reporting Experience working in global or cross-regional contexts Fluency in English (additional languages like Spanish or French are an advantage) Proficiency in Google Workspace Work Requirements Ability to work independently and collaboratively Availability to work within 6 hours of US Eastern Time Application Timeline Application Deadline: April 12, 2026 Fellowship Duration: 12 months Applicants are encouraged to apply early due to the competitive nature of the programme. How to Apply Interested candidates can submit their applications via the official CPJ careers portal: Apply for the CPJ Fellowship Why You Should Apply This fellowship offers a rare opportunity to: Work at the forefront of global press freedom advocacy Conduct meaningful research on climate-related risks to journalists Engage with global stakeholders and policymakers Build a strong career in international advocacy, journalism, or policy Learn more about the Climate Change and Press Freedom Fellowship 2026 HERE. Discover more global Journalist opportunities on OFY HERE and more climate opportunities HERE.
